questions.question: Explain the difference between a class and an object with a suitable example.
keyPoints: Class is a blueprint/template defining attributes and methods
Object is a concrete instance of a class
Example: Class 'Car' defines attributes like color, model, and methods like start(), stop()
Object 'myCar' is a specific car instance with actual values

questions.question: What is encapsulation? How does it help in data hiding?
keyPoints: Encapsulation bundles data and methods into a single unit (class)
Uses access modifiers (private, public, protected)
Private data can only be accessed through public methods (getters/setters)
This prevents unauthorized access and ensures data integrity

questions.question: Define inheritance and explain its benefits in OOP.
keyPoints: Inheritance allows a class to acquire properties of another class
Class acquiring properties is subclass/derived class
Class from which properties are acquired is superclass/base class
Benefits: Code reusability, extensibility, better organization

questions.question: What is polymorphism? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Polymorphism allows objects of different classes to be treated uniformly
Two types: Compile-time (overloading) and Runtime (overriding)
Example: Method area() works differently for Circle, Rectangle, Triangle
Same message/interface, different behavior based on object type

questions.question: Explain abstraction with a real-world example.
keyPoints: Abstraction hides complex implementation details
Shows only essential features to users
Real-world example: ATM machine - users see screen, keypad, buttons
Internal details of transaction processing are hidden from user

questions.question: What is a constructor in Java? Explain its types.
keyPoints: Constructor is a special method to initialize objects
Has same name as class, no return type
Types: Default (no-arg), Parameterized
Default constructor provided by compiler if none defined

questions.question: Write a Java class 'Student' with appropriate attributes, constructor, and a method to display student details.
keyPoints: Define class Student with attributes (name, rollNo, marks)
Define parameterized constructor to initialize attributes
Define display() method to show details
Main method to create object and test

questions.question: Explain the four fundamental concepts of Object-Oriented Programming in detail with real-world examples.
keyPoints: Encapsulation: Bundling data and methods (e.g., BankAccount class)
Inheritance: Acquiring properties (e.g., Animal-Dog relationship)
Polymorphism: Multiple forms (e.g., payment methods)
Abstraction: Hiding complexity (e.g., car controls)
Explain each with practical examples and significance

questions.question: Discuss the advantages of Object-Oriented Programming over Procedural Programming. Why is OOP preferred for large software development?
keyPoints: OOP models real-world entities better
Modularity: Independent, reusable components
Reusability: Inheritance promotes code reuse
Maintainability: Encapsulation makes updates easier
Extensibility: New features through inheritance
Data hiding: Protection through encapsulation
Better troubleshooting: Clear organization

questions.question: What is a class in Java? Explain the structure of a class with an example program showing data members, methods, and constructors.
keyPoints: Class as blueprint/template
Structure: fields/attributes, constructors, methods
Example with class definition
Demonstrate object creation using new keyword
Explain how fields store data and methods define behavior

questions.question: Explain the following OOP concepts with suitable Java code examples: (i) this keyword (ii) Constructors (iii) Method overloading
keyPoints: this: Reference to current object, distinguishes instance variables
Constructors: Initialize objects, types (default, parameterized)
Method overloading: Same method name, different parameters
Provide working Java code examples for each concept
